steps.title: Identify the slope
steps.content: For a linear function $g(x) = mx + b$, the difference $g(x+1) - g(x) = m$. Given $g(x+1) - g(x) = 2$, the slope is $m = 2$. So $g(x) = 2x + b$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• A: $g(x) = 9x + 2$ — slope 9, not 2; $g(9) = 83 e 11$.
  • B: $g(x) = 9x + 11$ — slope 9, not 2; $g(9) = 92 e 11$.
  • C: $g(x) = 2x - 18$ — correct slope but $g(9) = 0 e 11$.
  • D: $g(x) = 2x - 7$ — slope 2 and $g(9) = 11$.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
